extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 6 by timing a late outside slingshot around Kristoff from 8th position with ~200m remaining.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step overcame early positioning failure by stringing peloton at high tempo via Sabatini before final 200m, enabling Kittel's late jump.
Kittel exploited late roundabouts at 1km and 400m remaining which elongated sprint peloton, creating passing lanes despite poor position.
Dimension Data disrupted Quick-Step's control between 1km-400m remaining, forcing sprinters to navigate from deeper positions than typical leadout finishes.
Extreme heat (99°F/37°C) over long stage may have blunted competitors' acceleration reserves relative to Kittel's sustained power output.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Won second stage after terrible positioning on stage 4 crash day, suggesting Quick-Step learned tactical adjustments for chaotic finishes
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: Lost 13 points in green jersey despite second place, indicating insufficient sprint speed to challenge Kittel even with better positioning
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: Provided inadvertent slingshot for Kittel but closed Demare dangerously into barriers, revealing aggressive late-sprint tactics under pressure
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Commentary identifies him as green jersey threat via medium mountain breakaways, not sprint speed
